title: mentha gentilis subsp. pratensis
intro: Mentha gentilis subsp. pratensis, also known as wild mint or meadow mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. It is a perennial herb with a strong minty aroma and flavor, which is often used to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties. The essential oil extracted from Mentha gentilis subsp. pratensis has been shown to possess antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable remedy for digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems. The herb can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to alleviate symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
